steps.title: Spot the defining contrast
steps.content: The passage says the researchers expected the magnetic field of G47 to be similar to those in other galactic bones — which are presumably uniform. What surprised them was the opposite: the field had "no clear pattern or direction." The correct answer must capture that unexpected lack of uniformity.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "It showed a weaker magnetic field than expected.": The passage says nothing about field strength being surprising; the surprise was about pattern, not power. ✗
  • Choice B — "It implied that previous mappings of the magnetic field were inaccurate.": No previous mappings are mentioned; this is unsupported by the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — "It produced magnetic field measurements similar to those for other galactic bones.": This is the opposite of surprising — similarity to other bones was the expectation, not the finding. ✗
  • Choice D — "It revealed a magnetic field that wasn't uniform.": Directly matches "no clear pattern or direction," which was unexpected given the uniform fields found elsewhere. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
